name: Status Panel
description: A small wall-mounted display showing the status of the four pods in this bay. Two read STABLE. One reads VACANT. The fourth — yours — reads OCCUPANT REVIVED — UNSCHEDULED. A timestamp shows the revival was recent. Below that, a line reads BAY C-7 OF 12. DECK C HIBERNATION STATUS: NOMINAL.
secret: Tapping a specific sequence on the panel pages through to other bays’ status, revealing one bay with an anomalous reading that suggests its occupant revived long ago.

properties.takeRefusal: The panel is bolted to the wall.

name: Vapor Suit
description: A thin, form-fitting jumpsuit draped over the edge of your pod. Standard hibernation recovery wear — silver-gray with a faded mission patch on the shoulder. The patch reads AARU in block letters around a stylized reed.
secret: The suit has a hidden pocket in the lining that contains a small data chip.

name: Directory Console
description: A cracked but functional display showing a partial map of the ship's C-deck. Several sections are marked OFFLINE or RESTRICTED. You can make out labels for Hibernation, Medical, Hydroponics, Engineering Access, and something called the Commons that takes up a large area toward the bow.
secret: Touching the RESTRICTED areas on the display repeatedly causes a brief audio log to play — a fragment of an old security announcement.

properties.takeRefusal: The console is built into the pillar.
properties.dataContent:
C-DECK DIRECTORY (partial)
────────────────────
Hibernation Bays C1–C12 ... STABLE
Medical Station C ........ DEGRADED
Hydroponics Gallery ...... OPERATIONAL
Engineering Access C ..... RESTRICTED
The Commons .............. OFFLINE
Vertical Transit ......... DEGRADED
Cargo Bay C .............. UNKNOWN

name: Diagnostic Terminal
description: A terminal on a swivel arm connected to the diagnostic bed. It shows your vitals: heart rate, neural activity, blood chemistry. Everything reads normal except a field labeled IDENTITY MATRIX, which shows three red dashes. A prompt at the bottom reads: COGNITIVE ASSESSMENT AVAILABLE. TYPE 'BEGIN' TO START.
secret: The terminal is connected to the ship’s medical network — if its power source were rerouted it could access other patients’ records from across the ship.

properties.takeRefusal: The terminal is attached to the diagnostic bed.

name: Emergency Medkit
description: A compact white case with a red cross, sitting on the edge of the diagnostic bed. It looks like standard ship-issue emergency supplies.

name: Carved Figures
description: Dozens of tiny figures carved from what looks like reclaimed plastic and metal. They depict people in various poses — working, sleeping, dancing, arguing. Some are grouped together, others stand alone. The cloth beneath them is embroidered with a symbol you do not recognize: a circle divided into seven unequal parts.
secret: The figures can be rearranged on the cloth, and certain arrangements cause the nearby directory console to flicker and reveal hidden sections of the deck map.

properties.takeRefusal: You feel like you should not disturb the shrine.

name: Sweeper Unit
description: A knee-high disc-shaped robot with a single blue optical sensor and a rotating brush assembly underneath. It moves in slow, deliberate patterns across the junction floor, pausing occasionally to examine a spot of dust with what seems like genuine concern. A faded label reads CUSTODIAL UNIT 7. It has been decorated — someone tied a small ribbon around its antenna, and tiny carved beads dangle from its sensor housing.
secret: The sweeper unit has been cleaning this section for centuries and has witnessed everything — it is far more aware and intelligent than its simple custodial programming suggests.

properties.takeRefusal: The sweeper unit rolls away indignantly.

name: Cultivar Tags
description: Brass tags on every trough, engraved with the breeders' names for their work: SERENE ARGUMENT. THE LESSER APOLOGY. GRANDMOTHER'S SECOND OPINION. The plants do not seem embarrassed.

name: The Tender
description: A garden robot on a fixed circuit of the rows, secateurs for one hand and a misting wand for the other. Its chassis is wreathed in a vine it has decided not to prune, for reasons it has logged but not explained.
secret: The Tender has continued the breeding program alone for three hundred years and considers the Deep Growth its masterwork, though it will only call it 'the back rows.'

name: Glow Fruit
description: A fist-sized fruit lit gently from within, pulse matching the canopy overhead. Warm to hold, like a small lamp that grew.

name: Dust Cloths
description: Cloths over every table and bench, laid with care — corners squared, edges weighted. Lifting one feels like interrupting.

scenery.description: The corners are weighted with whatever was nearest: mugs, game pieces, one shoe. The weights were chosen in a hurry. The folding wasn't.
scenery.rejection: The cloths have kept their shapes for years. It seems rude to be the reason they stop.

name: The Steeper
description: A tea-stall robot with a polished kettle and an unpolished everything else. It maintains the stall, the ritual, and a guest book. It is very glad you are here, in the manner of something that has practiced saying so.
secret: The Steeper's guest book has one entry per day, every day, since the hibernation: 'No patrons. Tea was ready.' Today's entry is already amended.

name: Tea Bowl
description: A glazed bowl from the Steeper's matched set of four. The glaze is crazed with centuries of heat-cycles into something map-like.

name: Sump Wrench
description: A long-handled wrench worn bright at the grip. Stamped PUMP GALLERY — RETURN, with the calm authority of a tool that always has been.

name: Memory Coil
description: A palm-sized coil of bronze-colored wire, tagged: 'Personal recording, Fourth Era. Plays once per holder. Handle with sympathy.'

properties.dataContent: A voice, mid-laugh, explaining to a child why the stars don't move: '—because WE'RE the one that's going somewhere, little fish. Everything else already arrived.'

name: Launch-Day Helmet
description: A flight helmet from the day the Aaru left, visor polished by a thousand years of careful cloths. Inside the brim, a name has been worn away by exactly the same care.

name: The Catalog
description: The Reliquary's curator: a terminal on a brass column, lens raised, annotating. Its cursor blinks at the pace of something thinking about you specifically.
secret: The Catalog will trade provenance information for artifacts or stories — and it has an open accession slot reserved for the player.
